Zhou Mingrui.primary_clothing: everyday wear: brownish-yellow coat with matching felt hat with rounded edge; formal wear: black vest with matching suit, tight trousers, and halved top hat
Zhou Mingrui.visual_anchor: early 20s Chinese male, short black hair, dark brown eyes, wearing modern sleepwear
Zhou Mingrui.distinguishing_features: carries a brass revolver concealed in left coat pocket; keeps banknotes clasped in left palm pressing against the gun handle when walking through crowded streets; the halved top hat is a signature item for formal dress
Zhou Mingrui.source_notes: Text explicitly describes both outfit combinations and the revolver-concealment habit in detail.
Zhou Mingrui.era_clothing_lock: brownish-yellow wool coat with matching rounded-brim felt hat for daily errands; black vest and matching black suit with tight trousers and a vertically halved top hat for formal occasions — both fully Victorian in silhouette

Animal Trainer.primary_clothing: circus performer attire, practical and slightly worn
Animal Trainer.face_description: weathered face from traveling, sharp observant eyes
Animal Trainer.visual_anchor: female circus animal trainer in practical performer attire with a weathered face
Animal Trainer.color_palette: dusty brown, faded red, canvas white
Animal Trainer.distinguishing_features: shorter and stockier than the real circus fortune-teller; wears identical costume to the Tarot Reader but has a noticeably different physique
Animal Trainer.source_notes: Chapter 5 confirms grayish-blue eyes, full circus fortune-teller costume, and contrasts her shorter/stockier build against the taller/slimmer real fortune-teller. She is scolded for impersonating the Tarot Reader and told to return to her actual job as animal trainer.
Animal Trainer.era_clothing_lock: sharp pointy witch hat, floor-length black dress, face painted red and yellow (circus costume worn while impersonating the fortune-teller)

Tarot Reader.primary_clothing: pointed black hat, long black dress
Tarot Reader.face_description: face obscured by heavy red and yellow stage paint; striking grayish-blue eyes described as 'profound', suggesting depth and intensity
Tarot Reader.visual_anchor: woman of unknown age with profound grayish-blue eyes behind a face painted red and yellow, wearing a tall pointed black hat and floor-length Victorian black dress, operating a small divination tent at a circus square
Tarot Reader.distinguishing_features: face smeared with red and yellow paint matching the circus performers nearby; hoarse voice; sets up a short tent for divination at the municipal square circus
Tarot Reader.faction: circus / traveling performer; practices tarot divination
Tarot Reader.role: minor — carnival divination reader who offers Klein a free tarot reading
Tarot Reader.source_notes: Text explicitly describes: 'a woman wearing a pointed hat and a long black dress standing in front of a short tent. Her face was smeared with red and yellow paint and her eyes were a profound grayish-blue.' Voice described as hoarse.
Tarot Reader.era_clothing_lock: tall pointed witch-style black hat, full-length black dress in Victorian silhouette, face heavily painted with red and yellow stage paint

Roselle Gustav.face_description: handsome and charismatic face with an air of authority
Roselle Gustav.visual_anchor: middle-aged male, elaborate emperor attire, charismatic and authoritative presence
Roselle Gustav.color_palette: imperial red, gold, white, navy blue
Roselle Gustav.distinguishing_features: historical legend; credited with inventing steam engine, modern sailing improvements, tarot divination, and card game systems; led the era of Southern Continent colonialism via marine fleets
Roselle Gustav.faction: Intis Republic / Intis Empire (founder); recognized by God of Craftsmanship (later God of Steam and Machinery)
Roselle Gustav.role: historical figure — Intis Republic's Consul, later self-proclaimed Emperor Caesar; inventor of the steam engine, tarot divination, and contemporary card game systems; recognized by the God of Craftsmanship; assassinated in year 1198 of the Fifth Epoch at the White Maple Palace by combined forces of the Church of the Eternal Blazing Sun, the Sauron family (former Intis royal family), and aristocrats
Roselle Gustav.source_notes: Extended historical exposition in chapter text covering Roselle's full biography, titles, achievements, and assassination.

Benson Moretti.primary_clothing: Linen shirt, black coat, halved top hat
Benson Moretti.face_description: Resembles Klein but lacks the scholarly air, looks older than his actual age due to work stress.
Benson Moretti.visual_anchor: mid-20s male, pale skin, short black hair, wearing working class Victorian attire, showing signs of fatigue
Benson Moretti.distinguishing_features: literate, employed at an import and export company, resourceful and calculating — negotiated gas pipe installation to avoid debt
Benson Moretti.source_notes: Text states he 'worked for several years' and 'using the convenience of working at an import and export company, he purchased a brand new gas lamp which was nearly at cost price'

Audrey Hall.hair_style: long blond hair coiled in an elegant bun
Audrey Hall.primary_clothing: collared, high-waisted, pale-white dress with engageantes, tight corset, voluminous cage crinoline; white-silk gloves; white dancing shoes with roses and diamonds.
Audrey Hall.face_description: delicate and youthful features, a soft jawline, and large expressive eyes
Audrey Hall.visual_anchor: late teens female, fair skin, long blonde hair, wearing a luxurious Victorian aristocratic dress, holding a cracked bronze mirror
Audrey Hall.distinguishing_features: Lives in Backlund, Empress Borough in a large estate with maids, a balcony, and oil paintings on walls; practices Spectator state as a Beyonder
Audrey Hall.faction: Tarot Club (Justice); noble class resident of Backlund, Empress Borough
Audrey Hall.role: supporting — Tarot Club member, Sequence 8 Spectator; accidentally gave a Sequence 9 potion to an animal
Audrey Hall.source_notes: Chapter explicitly places Audrey in Backlund, Empress Borough. Her room has oil paintings, a down-feathered pillow, a maid, and a shaded balcony where Susie the golden retriever sits.

Alger Wilson.primary_clothing: Captain's uniform of the 'Blue Avenger', suited for sea travel.
Alger Wilson.face_description: Rough face with a stoic and solemn expression.
Alger Wilson.visual_anchor: early 30s male, weathered skin, dark messy hair, wearing a robe embroidered with lightning patterns, holding a quirky-shaped glass bottle
Alger Wilson.color_palette: deep sea blue, silver, dark grey
Alger Wilson.distinguishing_features: maintains effortless balance on a storm-tossed ship deck; robe marked with lightning embroidery is his signature garment
Alger Wilson.accessories: quirky-shaped glass bottle containing supernatural substances — bubbles, frost/snow, and gusting wind visible inside at different moments
Alger Wilson.source_notes: Chapter 5 explicitly describes Alger on the deck of a three-masted sailboat in a storm, wearing a lightning-patterned robe and holding a glass bottle with shifting supernatural contents (bubbles, frost, wind). He is seeking Ghost Shark's blood for an unknown purpose.
Alger Wilson.era_clothing_lock: robe embroidered with lightning patterns, worn aboard a three-masted sailboat at sea

The Celestial Worthy of Heaven and Earth.name_english: The Celestial Worthy of Heaven and Earth for Blessings
The Celestial Worthy of Heaven and Earth.name_bengali: দ্য সেলেস্টিয়াল ওয়ার্থি অফ হেভেন অ্যান্ড আর্থ ফর ব্লেসিংস
The Celestial Worthy of Heaven and Earth.hair_style: long, tied back in ancient style
The Celestial Worthy of Heaven and Earth.primary_clothing: Ornate ancient mystical robes with complex yellow and black patterns, wide sleeves
The Celestial Worthy of Heaven and Earth.face_description: Majestic and inscrutable face, deep-set eyes that seem to hold stars, calm and serene expression of authority.
The Celestial Worthy of Heaven and Earth.visual_anchor: Ancient majestic figure in yellow and black ornate robes, deep black eyes, long black hair, radiating an aura of primordial authority.
The Celestial Worthy of Heaven and Earth.distinguishing_features: Original source of the luck-enhancement ritual chant
The Celestial Worthy of Heaven and Earth.source_notes: Mentioned as the origin of the ritual chant translated by Klein.
